the three main pillar genres are definitely Adventure, Needle, Avoidance, with the three secondary pillar genres being Sudoku, Precision, and Trap (in my opinion this is not a standardized science i am not John Fangame)
a lot of needle is something insane happens then it becomes a new formula, but it still becomes a formula
and that goes for all of them
i do agree with you alice that it goes for all genres, but needle definitely gets less personality from its makers, which makes sense because of the amount that gets made
like doing that kinda stuff takes effort

i wonder now as a thought experiment if i could go through Kill The Needle Games and try and count unique makers based only off the save picks, i.e. seeing if i can identify makers based on needle design
